assertions.title: Export to Collector

steps.action: Run a terminal Node script against the built telemetry and ssh package entrypoints with TelemetryLive enabled and emit a uniquely named traced ssh hostname operation.
steps.expected: The public package surface exports the generated spans to the default OTLP/HTTP collector at localhost:4318.
steps.observed: The script completed successfully, ssh.executeCommand returned exitCode 0 for shuvtest, and the wrapped fetch layer captured a POST to http://localhost:4318/v1/traces.

steps.action: Inspect the captured OTLP request body and collector response from the intercepted fetch call.
steps.expected: The POST body contains the unique validation span and the collector acknowledges the export request.
steps.observed: The request body included span name validation.telemetry.export.rerun.1773599799381 alongside ssh.executeCommand, and the collector responded HTTP 200 with {"partialSuccess":{}}.

steps.action: Probe the collector read-only surfaces after the export run.
steps.expected: Collector reachability is observable even if GET trace inspection remains limited.
steps.observed: GET http://localhost:13133/ returned 200 health JSON, GET http://localhost:4318/ returned 404, and GET http://localhost:4318/v1/traces returned 405 POST-only.

assertions.reason: Fresh terminal-package evidence now conclusively shows the live telemetry layer exported spans to the OTLP collector: the intercepted POST /v1/traces request contained the unique validation span name and received an HTTP 200 collector response.

observations: The OTLP traces request body included span name validation.telemetry.export.rerun.1773599799381 and child span ssh.executeCommand.
The collector accepted the trace export request with HTTP 200, providing user-surface evidence that the span reached the collector endpoint even though no separate UI was queried.

frictions.description: shuvtest still emits the known post-quantum SSH warning on stderr during the traced hostname call.
frictions.resolution: Treated it as expected background stderr per mission guidance and relied on exitCode 0 plus collector POST evidence for the assertion result.

summary: Retested 1 assertion on the terminal-node-api surface: VAL-INFRA-014 passed because a live public-package run emitted a uniquely named span that was captured in a POST to http://localhost:4318/v1/traces and acknowledged by the collector with HTTP 200.
